text: Another. The angel who measured spoke to Michael. (Calmet)

Walls. This must be understood of the spiritual Jerusalem, the Church of Christ. (Challoner)<hr>According to St. Augustine (in Psalm lxxi.) when the literal sense cannot be verified, we must have recourse to the thing prefigured; and thus what is here written, must be explained of the Church rather than of Jerusalem. (Worthington)<hr>A little before the fall of the latter, it was become so populous that the houses which had been built without the walls were enclosed. (Josephus, Jewish Wars 6:6.)<hr>This multitude was a sort of pledge or figure of the crowds which should embrace the gospel.
Fire, to enlighten and protect it. Arms will be unnecessary, Micheas 5:10., and Isaias 2:4. The Church shall enjoy peace.
North. Many Jews had not yet returned, Esther, etc.<hr>Winds, Ezechiel 5:2, 12. (Calmet)<hr>Gentiles, and ye children of Sion, flee from Babylon and from sin into the true Church. (Worthington)

Glory. After restoring you to your own country, and rebuilding your cities, I will punish your enemies. (Calmet)<hr>After they have enjoyed prosperity, they shall feel the reverse. Hebrew Cabod, (Haydock) means also "a burden."

Prey. Two years after this (Calmet) the Assyrians revolted, and seized Babylon. (Justin i.)<hr>They slew the useless women: but Zopyrus betrayed the place to Darius, who hung 3,000 of the principal inhabitants, and demolished the walls. (Herodotus 3:150.; Usher, year of the world 3489.)

Nations. We know of none who embraced the Jewish law. But both the old and the new world submits to Christ.<hr>Dwell. St. Michael represents the Messias. The latter preached and wrought miracles among the Jews, which rendered them inexcusable, John 15:24. (Calmet)
Silent: obey with reverence. (Haydock) See Habacuc 2:20., and 1 Machabees 1:3. (Calmet)<hr>Habitation, becoming man. (Menochius)
